title: Patient Identification
description: Confirm patient identity and referral source
reasoning: Essential for safety and record accuracy

title: Chief Complaint Documented
reasoning: Primary presenting symptom identified and characterised

title: Medication History
reasoning: Critical — identified supratherapeutic acetaminophen and Augmentin as hepatotoxins

title: Acetaminophen Dose Quantified
reasoning: Key finding — supratherapeutic dosing is leading cause of acute liver failure

title: GI Symptoms Assessed
reasoning: Supports hepatic involvement with capsular stretch

title: Cholestasis Markers
description: Dark urine, pale stools, pruritus documented
reasoning: Obstructive pattern suggests cholestatic component — supports Augmentin DILI

title: Exposure Risk Screening
description: Travel, contacts, IV drugs, tattoos assessed

title: Encephalopathy Screen
description: No confusion or mental status changes
reasoning: Important negative — no hepatic encephalopathy at this stage

title: Physical Examination
description: Liver palpation and scleral icterus confirmation
reasoning: Bedside assessment needed to complement history

title: Urgent Bloods Ordered
description: LFTs, coagulation, paracetamol level, hepatitis serology
reasoning: Essential for diagnosis confirmation and severity grading
